confirmed.title: uv.lock out of sync with declared version bounds (pytest, pytest-cov)
confirmed.verification: Verified through code inspection: /Users/thomasjones/hc-grc/uv.lock contains pytest 9.0.3 (line 317) and pytest-cov 7.1.0 (line 333), which violate declared bounds in requirements.txt:100-101 (pytest>=8.2.2,<9, pytest-cov>=5.0.0,<6), pyproject.toml:41-42 (same bounds in [dev]), and requirements-ci.txt:31-32 (same). uv.lock was created once in pass-2 (commit 2027513, 2026-06-13 09:31:50) and never updated; bounds were tightened in pass-11 (commit 6debed1, 2026-06-15). Testing with 'uv sync --dry-run' confirms it would install pytest==9.0.3 and pytest-cov==7.1.0. However, severity is LOW because: (1) CI explicitly uses 'pip install -r requirements-ci.txt' per .github/workflows/test.yml:51, not uv; (2) project documentation (README.md, CONTRIBUTING_DOCS.md, etc.) never mentions uv as a supported installation method; (3) uv.lock is tracked in git but there is no documented pathway instructing users to use it. The defect is REAL (violates CI/prod parity principle) but has minimal practical impact due to lack of documented uv usage.
confirmed.recommended_action: Update uv.lock to resolve pytest to >=8.2.2,<9 and pytest-cov to >=5.0.0,<6 by running 'uv sync' in the repository and committing the result. This ensures consistency across all three installation mechanisms (pip/requirements-ci.txt, pip/pyproject.toml[dev], and uv/uv.lock), maintaining the CI/prod parity principle established in pass-2. Alternatively, remove uv.lock from version control and document that pip with requirements.txt or requirements-ci.txt is the canonical installation method, if uv is not a supported installation path going forward. Option 1 is preferred for consistency.
confirmed.proposed_fix: Either: (1) Update uv.lock to resolve pytest to 8.x and pytest-cov to 5.x by running 'uv sync' and committing the result, or (2) Remove uv.lock from version control and document that pip/requirements-ci.txt is the canonical installation path for CI and development. Option 1 is preferred if uv is a supported installation method; option 2 if pip is the sole supported mechanism.
